name: snappy-manifolds

metadata.name: snappy-manifolds
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 or later (GPLv2+)

documentation:
# The SnapPy manifold database

This repository stores the core manifold databases that come with
SnapPy, and includes the source code for the Python module
"snappy_manifolds" which packages them up for use in SnapPy and
Spherogram.

The raw source for the tables are in:

```
manifold_src/original_manifold_sources
```

stored as plain text CSV files for the potential convenience of other
users. The triangulations themselves are stored in the "isosig" format
of Burton, as described in the appendix to [this paper](http://arxiv.org/abs/1110.6080) with an added "decoration" suffix
that describes the peripheral framing.

modules.name: snappy_manifolds.sqlite_files
documentation: The submodule containing the actual databases of manifolds

modules.name: snappy_manifolds.test
documentation:
We do only a very basic test for this module, since most of the
doctests are run by snappy.

```pycon
>>> Rolfsen, HT = get_DT_tables()
>>> len(Rolfsen), len(HT)
(1216, 180511)
>>> str(HT['L12n345'])
'lbbjceGkjHILFadb.010001100011'
```

functions.name: snappy_manifolds.sqlite_files.make_sqlite_db.make_table
documentation:
Given a csv of manifolds data and a connection to a sqlite database,
insert the data into a new table. If the csv file is in a subdirectory
of the csv directory csv_dir, it is given by sub_dir.

functions.name: snappy_manifolds.database.get_core_tables
documentation:
Functions such as this one are meant to be called in the
__init__.py module in snappy proper.  To avoid circular imports,
it takes as argument the class ManifoldTable from database.py in
snappy. From there, it builds all of the Manifold tables from the
sqlite databases manifolds.sqlite and more_manifolds.sqlite in
manifolds_src, and returns them all as a list.

functions.name: snappy_manifolds.database.get_tables
documentation:
This function is meant to be called in the __init__.py module in
snappy proper. To avoid circular imports, it takes as argument the
class ManifoldTable from database.py in snappy. From there, it
builds all of the Manifold tables from the sqlite databases
manifolds.sqlite and more_manifolds.sqlite in manifolds_src, and
returns them all as a list.

functions.name: snappy_manifolds.database.connect_to_db
documentation: Open the given sqlite database, ideally in read-only mode.

functions.name: snappy_manifolds.database.get_DT_tables
documentation: Returns two barebones databases for looking up DT codes by name.
